source_file: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.docx

paragraphs.text: Financial due diligence
The deliverable will be in the form of a report and Excel data book.

Unless otherwise noted, our work will concentrate on financial information for the last three fiscal years ended December 31, 2023, 2024, along with the most recent available financial information (e.g. year-to-date March 31, 2025 or more recent monthly data as available).
Quality of earnings

Analyze historical earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (“EBITDA”) at segment levels including understanding the impact of potential non-recurring or non-operation items such as:
Obtain and comment on the Seller’s proposed adjustments analysis;
Working with Client, understand potential impact with potential change in incentive compensation, dis-synergies, or synergies (if applicable);
Understand the impact of the Company’s JV business in the dredging sector;
The impact of significant accounting policies, provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
Other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
Non-cash items such as the impact of provisions, management estimates, or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.
Supporting analysis to quality of earnings
Sales and margin trends
To the extent available, obtain and read an analysis of Target’s revenues and margins and inquire about the following:
Analyze revenue, margin, pricing and average project length on a project basis by the following (where available):
Revenue stream (civil work, special projects (buildings), services and equipment rental);
Customer
Geography
Industry
Understand Target’s job costing methodology (components included in margin, and periodic true ups)

Understand Target’s revenue recognition and billing process (e.g. percentage of completion, periodic and contract completion true-ups (and its variability), loss provision process, policies in place to control accounting recognition and policy escalations, and lifecycle), with specific focus on Construction Management contracts, and its corresponding timing impact to EBITDA;
Understanding the profile (and exposure) of major project/contracts (i.e. Indigenous Partnership with C&C Horizon Contracting Ltd.);
Obtain and analyze the backlog and active backlog as at latest available date (backlog margin on a project basis, and analysis of historical backlog conversion)
Cost of sales and operating costs
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's expenses and inquire about:
Costing methodology;
Cost of sale trends, impact of material change in vendors (if any);
Employee compensation and related costs by function;
Selling, general, and administrative expenses;
Repairs and maintenance expense;
Unusual and extraordinary items (if any).
Working Capital
Obtain and read an analysis of working capital and inquire about the following on a combined basis:
Understand the composition of individual working capital accounts to determine sufficient working capital needs of the Company (including bonding requirements);
Monthly trending and fluctuations in working capital and impact on reported metrics (e.g. days sales/payables/inventory outstanding, net working capital as a percentage of revenues, etc.);

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current or upcoming initiatives that may affect normalized working capital trends, such as exited business, settlements, capital projects, understanding non-trade balances, etc.
Gain an understanding of the nature of the Company’s unearned revenue.
Supporting analysis to net working capital
Accounts receivable
Obtain and read an analysis of the Target’s accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:
Billed/unbilled aging analysis, turnover and days sales outstanding;
Credit terms;
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs.
Prepaid materials and other assets
Prepare a summary of other assets (including prepaid materials, prepaid assets) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances
Inventory
Prepare a summary of inventory and obtain an understanding of the Company’s inventory costing methodology, tracking of inventory, work-in-progress and obsolescence reserve.
Accounts payable and accrued liabilities
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:
Terms of trade with major suppliers, significant vendor allowances, rebates and/or purchase commitments;
Trade payables aging analysis and days payable outstanding analysis;
Accrued liabilities;
Billings in excess of cost profile; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.
Fixed asset equipment and vehicles
Obtain an understanding of the Company’s current equipment, including:
Mix of owned versus leased equipment/ vehicles;
Average age of equipment/ vehicles;
For leased fleet, understand the annual payment requirements;
For owned fleet, obtain details of historical capital expenditures and comment on planned, committed, and deferred capital expenditures.
Net debt and other assets/liabilities

Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, deferred revenue, pension deficit, warranty matters, exposure from onerous contracts, contingent matters, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.
Summary of lease right-of-use assets and liabilities.
Related parties

Inquire about Target’s transactions (e.g. income statement and balance sheet impacts) with related parties including subsidiaries, holding companies, joint ventures, variable interest entities, and other entities under common ownership.
Commitments and contingencies
Obtain and comment on any contingent claims or liabilities including:
Contractual obligations, including leases;
Pending or threatened litigation or investigation by regulatory or other authorities; and
Other off-balance sheet transactions (including contingencies and guarantees).
Understanding Target’s accounting and financial reporting environment
Accounting overview
Understand and summarize the financial statement preparation process, including:

Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/ accounting systems);
Integration of recent acquisitions and status of any ongoing integration processes;
Significant accounts estimates and any recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates.
If available, understand the reviewed statements unrecorded differences, management letter points, and other significant findings encountered during the annual review.
Accounting comparison (IFRS vs ASPE)
Understanding Target’s accounting and financial reporting environment
Target’s accounting policies and the key differences with IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Bird Construction

Analyze the accounting policies as disclosed in the audited financial statements and perform a high-level gap assessment to identify differences between the Target’s accounting policies and practices and IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Bird Construction Inc.

(Following the high-level gap assessment, if a more detailed gap assessment is required, we will work with Client to identify topics for further analysis and the estimate fees for the incremental analysis.)
Synergy Diligence

The objective is to quantify the level of synergies available from this transaction. Our primary focus is to develop a defendable and robust case supported by clear rationale and strong evidence. We can produce the synergy case independently or in collaboration with your team by reviewing, challenging, and identifying upsides.
Understand the future operating model, key integration assumptions and main constraints through documentation review and meetings with Target Management and Client;
Develop a high-level Target Operating model by area, functions, and activity, indicating opportunity for synergies;
Perform external functional benchmarking of other players in the industry and related industries from publicly available information, KPMG’s previous engagements and from KPMG proprietary Database;

Develop assumptions, rationale and calculations supporting various synergy initiatives and perform bottom-up cost modelling complemented by a top down assessment, considering on-going synergies/dis-synergies and economies of scale in the areas such as:
Executive / senior team;
Back office / G&A FTE and non-FTE (HR, Finance, and Marketing / Sales);
Technology;
Outsourced providers;
Footprint / facilities rationalization; and
Sourcing and procurement synergies for direct & indirect costs.
High-level (quantified where possible) of one-time savings/costs; and
Provide overall summary and supporting report of KPMG’s estimate of synergies and propose a range of adjustments to the Quality of Earnings, highlighting additional key risks and upsides.
Canadian Tax Due Diligence

Our Canadian tax due diligence procedures will be performed with respect to the proposed investment in Fraser River Pile & Dredge (GP) Inc. (the “Target”). We understand that Client may acquire other entities within the Target group. Our scope and fees will be refined upon review of the corporate structure and through discussions with you.
Our scoping procedures below are applicable to each acquired corporation.

Unless otherwise specifically noted below, our scope of work will be restricted to the periods FY20-FY24 and year to date 2025 (“YTD25”) (hereinafter termed as “period under investigation"). References to accounts and associated data are for these periods.
Our deliverable will be a Key Findings Report, in PowerPoint format, on significant potential tax exposures, and recommendations to mitigate the identified exposures as appropriate.
We will also comment on potential limitations identified regarding the future availability of tax attributes and related matters.
General
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about:
Overview of Canadian operations and legal structure;
Structuring of significant transactions (including strategies, previous acquisitions, dispositions, and reorganizations) and tax treatment thereof;
Nature and extent of tax planning arrangements including but not limited to a tax planning arrangement involving redeemable preferred shares as disclosed in the Target’s financial statements;
Nature of advances made to and received from related parties, any underwater intercorporate debt and potential debt forgiveness considerations;
Tax treatment of contract losses and unearned revenue;
Canadian subsidies and other relief measures pursued if any, quantum of subsidies received, and tax advice obtained (if applicable);
Any current or expected limitations with respect to interest deductions of Target;
Results of tax audits including jurisdictions subject to audit, quantum of known or proposed audit adjustments and, status of current/pending audits; and
Historical tax positions taken including the quantum of reserves for uncertain tax positions included in the financial statements and the basis for those reserves.
Obtain and read consolidated and standalone financial statements for all periods under investigation.
Obtain and read correspondence with tax authorities in respect of significant completed, ongoing and pending tax audits.
Obtain and read correspondence from internal and external tax advisors with respect to significant tax matters, as made available to us.

Read all pre-closing vendor-side reorganization or distribution plans including legal documents, steps memos, and compliance elements to the extent such pre-closing reorganizations impact Bird’s or Target’s exposure profile.
Corporation Income Tax and Withholding Taxes

Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about Target’s income tax compliance status, including internal procedures related to income tax compliance, and use of external tax preparers and advisors.

Obtain and read Canadian corporate tax returns for the period under investigation, filed by Target including legal entity financial statements, supporting work papers, notices of assessment, statement of account and other relevant information, as made available to us.
Obtain and read copies of tax due diligence reports for the acquisitions completed in the period under investigation.

Obtain and read schedules of tax basis of significant assets of Target and unused loss carry overs by jurisdiction (if any), including date of expiration and applicable restrictions on use. Inquire with Management and/or their tax advisors about existing restrictions on utilization of significant tax losses and other tax attributes.
Consider implications of an acquisition of control of Target on the availability of unused losses, and tax basis of significant assets.
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about domestic transfer pricing methodologies and supporting contemporaneous documentation.
Obtain and read any documents pertaining to intercompany asset charges, rent charges, management fees, as applicable.
Obtain and read documentation pertaining to the Excessive Interest and Financing Expenses Limitation (“EIFEL”) analysis undertaken by Target, if any.
Indirect Tax
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about:
Target’s indirect tax compliance status, including internal procedures related to indirect tax compliance;
Indirect tax status of Target’s revenue, e.g. taxable/non-taxable and exempt supplies;
Target’s PST self-assessment procedures, including whether PST has been self-assessed on any purchases/imports in the period under investigation; and
Restrictions on recovery of input tax credits.
Obtain and read all GST/HST/QST/PST returns and remittances for the period under investigation and supporting working papers.
Employment Taxes and CEWS
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about the following for the period under investigation:
Target’s payroll tax compliance status, including internal procedures related to payroll tax compliance;
Nature and extent of cross-border travel of employees and related tax compliance procedures;
Compliance history for Canadian payroll tax;
Payments to key employees that are contingent on closing of the transaction;
Details of employee incentive and profit-sharing plans and related payroll tax compliance; and
The nature and extent of use of individual independent contractors.

Obtain and read the summary of CEWS applications filed by Target and supporting documentation and make relevant inquiries in connection with procedures to determine eligibility and accuracy of calculations.
s
Tax Advisory Services (Optional)

Where requested by Client, we may also provide tax modeling and tax structuring assistance.  Such work is outside scope of the tax due diligence and subject to additional professional fees and is based on the actual time incurred at our agreed hourly rates.
The following list represents the most common structuring procedures encountered in transactions of this type.
At your request, we will assist with one or more of the following:

Assistance with tax input to the proposed acquisition structure, and commentary on acquiring, holding and exiting from the investment in a tax effective manner, including acquisition of control rules and associated implications to tax attributes.

Read initial draft of the Purchase Agreement and offer commentary to Client and its legal counsel concerning sections relating to Canadian tax matters based on the results of the due diligence assistance we provide.
Provide advice, consultations, calculations, or other services with respect to:
Pre-closing tax structuring, if any;
Tax deductibility of transaction costs, unamortized deferred finance fees, etc.;
Tax deductibility of intercompany management fees or other charges, if any;
Tax deductibility of interest expense on debt;
The income and withholding tax consequences of pushing down related or unrelated debt for tax purposes;
Assisting with post-closing transaction compliance and tax integration matters; and,
Pre- and post-transaction work with employee incentive plans, if any.
Prepare a high-level strawman and relevant tax considerations with respect to the chosen structure and relevant tax implications, if required.
Any other related tax advice or assistance with respect to the tax consequences of the proposed transaction or activities post-acquisition.
Any other related tax advice or assistance with respect to the tax consequences of the proposed transaction or activities post-acquisition.
